title: Deterministic alert layer for the live book (+ optional event-driven LLM triage)
source: CEO question 2026-05-29: should an agent monitor the live book? Conclusion: deterministic alerts + OPTIONAL event-driven LLM triage; never an LLM in the risk path.
why: The 60s kill-switch handles RISK REACTION (deterministic, must stay LLM-free). What is missing is ALERTING: a human is not always watching the dashboard, and a stale heartbeat / failed flatten / cap no longer binding can go unnoticed. A cheap deterministic threshold layer over the persisted state -> push notification covers ~80% of the value reliably; an OPTIONAL LLM triage fires ONLY on an alert (cron-bounded, read-only, explains + recommends, exits) for natural-language context and unknown-unknowns.
scope: Deterministic rules over signals/live/survival_book/*.json + logs: heartbeat stale (risk_state.json mtime), dd_latched tripped, drawdown approaching max_dd_stop, daily-loss approaching portfolio_kill, gross_leverage near cap, margin_level low, crypto variance share over cap, any [ALARM]/flatten_failed in stderr.
Emit via PushNotification; throttle/dedupe so it does not spam.

OPTIONAL phase 2: event-driven LLM triage (CronCreate-bounded, read-only) that runs ONLY when an alert trips -> plain-language explanation + recommended human action. NEVER acts on the broker (that is the B0098 command-intent path, separately gated). CEO opt-in (token cost).
NOT in the risk-reaction path; the deterministic kill-switch remains the sole fast actor.

history.reason: backlog: B0103 deterministic alert layer + optional event-driven LLM triage
feat(watchdog): B0103 â€” event-driven survival-book watchdog (deterministic + LLM)
chore: mark B0103 in-progress + clear smoke-test watchdog finding
chore(backlog): commit_sync hook â€” B0103 history

Deterministic alert layer completed (second wave over the existing watchdog core): (1) live_loop persists armed_limits {max_dd_stop, portfolio_kill, crypto_var_cap} in both diagnostics sites so approaching-rules use the REAL armed values (critical for the 25-jun portfolio_kill=0.055 envelope), state_reader exposes risk.armed_limits; (2) new rules: DD_LATCH_FIRED (HIGH, coherent latch), DD_APPROACHING (HIGH at >=80% of budget), DAILY_LOSS_APPROACHING (HIGH at >=80%, default-fallback tagged 'assumed default'), MARGIN_LOW (HIGH<300%/MED<1000%, only with margin in use), CRYPTO_VAR_OVER_CAP (MED, 5% tolerance); a LEVERAGE_NEAR_CAP rule was evaluated and REJECTED - a vol-targeted book operates AT its ceiling when the gate binds, so near-cap is the normal state (documented in code); (3) execution/notify.py - deterministic human push: Windows toast (PS 5.1 WinRT, fail-soft) + append-only signals/watchdog/notifications.log, wired into watchdog_monitor BEFORE the LLM launch and sharing decide_launches' edge-trigger/cooldown (no spam channel of its own), --no-toast flag. Verified end-to-end on the workstation: injected tick -> toast=ok + log line + dry LLM launch. 16 new tests (35 total watchdog) green. LLM stays OUT of the risk path; live service untouched (armed_limits picks up on next service start). Optional phase-2 LLM triage already existed (survival-watchdog skill).
